title: Self-mock 1: pick a Week-16 case and rework + DSA review
focus: Run a 60-min self-mock on a classic case (recsys/search/ads/fraud/ETA/feed).

items.label: 7-step framework: clarify → metrics → data → model → infra → eval → edge cases
interviewQuestions: Walk me through your 7-step framework for any ML system design interview.
How do you avoid running out of time on the model section?

items.label: Clarifying questions: scope, scale, latency, constraints
interviewQuestions: What are the first five clarifying questions you ask in any ML system design interview?
How do you confirm the business metric vs the ML metric without burning 10 minutes on it?

items.label: Online vs offline metrics, business metrics
interviewQuestions: How do you map a business metric to an offline ML metric?
Walk through three real cases where offline gains didn't translate online.

items.label: Design YouTube/TikTok feed recommendations
interviewQuestions: Walk me through designing TikTok's For You feed end-to-end.
How would you handle the cold-start problem for a new user?
What candidate-generation strategies would you use, and how do you combine them?

items.label: Two-stage candidate generation + ranking
interviewQuestions: Why do industrial recsys use a two-stage (recall + rank) architecture?
How do you avoid training/serving skew between recall and ranking?

items.label: Clarify requirements, users, constraints, and business metric for: pick a Week-16 case and rework
interviewQuestions: What are the first five clarifying questions before proposing any model?
What is the business metric, what is the ML proxy metric, and where can they diverge?
What scale, latency, freshness, privacy, and reliability constraints change the architecture?

items.label: Defend data, labels, baseline, model choice, feature freshness, and serving path for: pick a Week-16 case and rework
interviewQuestions: What baseline would you launch before a complex model, and how would you know it is insufficient?
Where do labels come from, what bias do they contain, and how delayed are they?
Which features must be computed online vs offline, and what happens when the feature store is slow?

items.label: Cover monitoring, rollback, cost, security, failure modes, and launch plan for: pick a Week-16 case and rework
interviewQuestions: What online metrics, guardrails, and cohort slices decide whether this system is safe to ramp?
What is the rollback trigger, fallback experience, and incident owner if quality or latency regresses?
What are the top security, privacy, abuse, or cost risks, and what controls would you ship in v1?

items.label: Rotate classic big-tech cases: feed, search, ads, fraud, ETA, notification ranking, marketplace matching
interviewQuestions: Pick one case and answer it in a Meta-style 45 minute ML design format.
What follow-up would a senior interviewer ask about feature freshness, p99 latency, and rollback?
What industry blog or product surface would you study before interviewing for that company?

items.label: Rotate startup cases: RAG API, anomaly detection, forecasting, model migration, multi-tenant AI platform
interviewQuestions: How would you scope the smallest credible MVP for this case in one week?
What would you buy or outsource first, and what would you keep custom?
What metric proves the first version is useful enough to keep funding?

references.label: Patrick Halina — ML Systems Design Interview Guide
Chip Huyen — Machine Learning Systems Design
Google Cloud Professional ML Engineer exam guide
Google ML system design mock interview — YouTube recommendations
Reddit — ML system design prep discussion
